intended_use: The TriVerity test is an automated, semi-quantitative in vitro diagnostic test that measures the relative expression levels of host response genes in RNA isolated from whole blood collected in the PAX
intended_use_full: The TriVerity test is an automated, semi-quantitative in vitro diagnostic test that measures the relative expression levels of host response genes in RNA isolated from whole blood collected in the PAXgene Blood RNA tube using reverse transcription loop-mediated isothermal amplification (RT-LAMP) on the Myrna instrument. The TriVerity test is indicated for use in conjunction with clinical assessments and other laboratory findings as an aid to differentiate bacterial infections, viral infections, and non-infectious illness, as well as to determine the likelihood of 7-day need for mechanical ventilation, vasopressors, and/or renal replacement therapy in adult patients with suspected acute infection or suspected sepsis presenting to the emergency department.
target_condition: suspected acute infection or suspected sepsis
other_metrics: Analytical sensitivity: 1x10^6 cp/mL (95% detection rate); Limit of Detection/Quantitation: 500 cells/µL; Reproducibility (multi-site): standard deviations ranged from 0.40 to 5.21 across bacterial/viral/severity scores; Intra-site precision: overall standard deviations of 2.71 (bacterial), 2.89 (viral), and 2.93 (severity); Linearity: all 32 markers linear from 1x10^6 to 1x10^9 copies/mL with Pearson correlation ≥0.943 and Allowable Deviation for Linearity ≤5%
